model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The previous AdamW+cosine configuration regressed, so switch back to Adam and remove scheduling while keeping a moderately higher LR than baseline to improve early optimization over the 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size doubles examples seen within the same 256 updates, which can improve validation loss for this undertrained MLP without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size increased to 128, a proportional LR increase can improve optimization progress within the fixed 256-step budget while keeping the successful configuration otherwise unchanged.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed 256-step training, doubling batch size again increases total seen examples to roughly one full epoch, which is likely to reduce validation loss more than small optimizer tweaks.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed update count, increasing to batch 512 further raises sample coverage per run, which may continue improving this MLP’s loss under the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling has consistently improved fixed-step performance so far; increasing to 1024 should further increase sample coverage per update budget while keeping the training setup otherwise unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Scaling batch size has repeatedly improved loss in fixed-step mode; moving to 2048 should further increase effective data coverage per optimizer step while preserving the currently strong optimizer and architecture settings.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After scaling to very large batches, increasing Adam learning rate can recover update magnitude and improve optimization speed within the fixed 256-step limit.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR increase to 2e-3 slightly regressed, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the strongest observed setting before testing other axes.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After large gains from more sample coverage, the next likely limiter is model capacity; increasing base width should improve representation power while keeping architecture simple.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With optimization now stable and data coverage high, adding one extra hidden layer should increase function capacity and may reduce validation loss further.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Depth increase helped, so a moderate width increase is the next simple capacity expansion likely to improve fit under the same fixed-step training budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Capacity scaling has been consistently beneficial; another moderate width increase is a low-complexity follow-up likely to continue improving validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Revert to the stronger width setting and test cosine decay across fixed steps, which may improve final validation loss by reducing late-step over-updates after rapid early progress.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The cosine schedule regressed, so revert it and test slightly weaker weight decay to reduce underfitting in the larger/deeper MLP setting.","secondary_modes":["summaries","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"A small additional reduction in weight decay may further ease underfitting in this high-capacity, large-batch regime while keeping the rest of the validated setup fixed.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Weight decay reductions have consistently helped; testing zero decay is a direct final-step extension to check whether regularization is still limiting this large-batch configuration.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With weight decay removed, a slight LR reduction may improve final-step stability and yield a small validation-loss gain without changing the successful architecture/batch setup.","secondary_modes":["caching","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The 9e-4 trial was slightly worse, so return to 1e-3 to recover the best observed setting for the final step.","secondary_modes":["caching","layout"]}
